title: Iterated integrals
description: Solve for the value of an iterated integral with numeric bounds.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-2}^{1} \left( \int_0^3 2x^2 + y^2 - 8xy^3 \, dx \right) dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_{-2}^{1} \left( \int_0^3 2x^2 + y^2 - 8xy^3 \, dx \right) dy &= \int_{-2}^1 \left[ \dfrac{2}{3}x^3 + y^2x - 4x^2y^3 \right]_0^3 dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^1 18 + 3y^2 - 36y^3 \, dy
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_{-2}^{1} \left( \int_0^3 2x^2 + y^2 - 8xy^3 \, dx \right) dy = 198$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_1^3 \left( \int_{-1}^1 4x^3 - 3xy^2 - 1 \, dx \right) dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\int_1^3 \left( \int_{-1}^1 4x^3 - 3xy^2 - 1 \, dx \right) dy &= \int_1^3 \left[ x^4 - \dfrac{3x^2y^2}{2} - x \right]_{-1}^1 dy \\ \\
&= \int_1^3 -2 \, dy
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\int_1^3 -2 \, dy &= -2y \bigg|_1^3 = -4
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_1^3 \left( \int_{-1}^1 4x^3 - 3xy^2 - 1 \, dx \right) dy = -4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-2}^0 \left( \int_0^1 xy + x^2 - y^2 \, dy \right) dx =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\int_{-2}^0 \left( \int_0^1 xy + x^2 - y^2 \, dy \right) dx &= \int_{-2}^0 \left[ \dfrac{xy^2}{2} + x^2y - \dfrac{y^3}{3} \right]_0^1 dx \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^0 \dfrac{x}{2} + x^2 - \dfrac{1}{3} \, dx
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\int_{-2}^0 \dfrac{x}{2} + x^2 - \dfrac{1}{3} \, dx &= \dfrac{x^2}{4} + \dfrac{x^3}{3} - \dfrac{x}{3} \bigg|_{-2}^0 \\ \\
&= 0 - \left( \dfrac{4}{4} - \dfrac{8}{3} - \dfrac{-2}{3} \right) \\ \\
&= -1 + \dfrac{6}{3} \\ \\
&= 1
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_{-2}^0 \left( \int_0^1 xy + x^2 - y^2 \, dy \right) dx = 1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \left( \int_{-2}^2 e^x + e^y \, dy \right) dx =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \left( \int_{-2}^2 e^x + e^y \, dy \right) dx &= \int_{-1}^1 \left[ ye^x + e^y \right]_{-2}^2 \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 2e^x + e^2 - \left( -2e^x + e^{-2} \right) \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 4e^x + e^2 - e^{-2} \, dx
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \left( \int_{-2}^2 e^x + e^y \, dy \right) dx = 2e^2 + 4e - 4e^{-1} - 2e^{-2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{3}^5 \left( \int_{2}^4 ye^{4x} \, dy \right) dx =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_3^5 \left( \int_2^4 ye^{4x} \, dy \right) dx &= \int_3^5 \left[ \dfrac{y^2e^{4x}}{2} \right]_2^4 dx \\ \\
&= \int_3^5 e^{4x} \left( \dfrac{16}{2} - \dfrac{4}{2} \right) dx \\ \\
&= \int_3^5 6e^{4x} dx
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_3^5 6e^{4x} dx &= \dfrac{6e^{4x}}{4} \bigg|_3^5 \\ \\
&= \dfrac{3}{2} (e^{20} - e^{12})
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_{3}^5 \left( \int_{2}^4 ye^{4x} \, dy \right) dx = \dfrac{3}{2} (e^{20} - e^{12})$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^2 \left( \int_0^2 x^3 e^y \, dx \right) dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_{-1}^2 \left( \int_0^2 x^3 e^y \, dx \right) dy &= \int_{-1}^2 \left[ \dfrac{x^4e^y}{4} \right]_0^2 dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^2 4e^y \, dy
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_{-1}^2 4e^y \, dy &= 4e^{y} \bigg|_{-1}^2 \\ \\
&= 4(e^2 - e^{-1})
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^2 \left( \int_0^2 x^3 e^y \, dx \right) dy = 4(e^2 - e^{-1})$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^\pi \left( \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos(x)\sin(y) \, dx \right) dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_0^\pi \left( \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos(x)\sin(y) \, dx \right) dy &= \int_0^\pi \left[ \sin(x)\sin(y) \right]_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_0^\pi \sin(y) \, dy
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_0^\pi \sin(y) \, dy &= -\cos(y) \bigg|_0^\pi \\ \\
&= 2
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_0^\pi \left( \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos(x)\sin(y) \, dx \right) dy = 2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-\pi}^0 \left( \int_0^{2\pi} \cos(2x) - 2\sin(y) \, dx \right) dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle \int_{-\pi}^0 \left( \int_0^{2\pi} \cos(2x) - 2\sin(y) \, dx \right) dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-\pi}^0 \left[ \dfrac{\sin(2x)}{2} - 2x\sin(y) \right]_0^{2\pi} dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-\pi}^0 -4\pi \sin(y) \, dy
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_{-\pi}^0 -4\pi \sin(y) \, dy &= 4\pi\cos(y) \bigg|_{-\pi}^0 \\ \\
&= 4\pi( \cos(0) - \cos(-\pi) ) \\ \\
&= 8\pi
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_{-\pi}^0 \left( \int_0^{2\pi} \cos(2x) - 2\sin(y) \, dx \right) dy = 8\pi$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^\pi \left( \int_2^{4} xy\cos(y) \, dx \right) dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^\pi \left( \int_2^{4} xy\cos(y) \, dx \right) dy \\ \\
&= \int_0^\pi \left[ \dfrac{x^2y\cos(y)}{2} \right]_2^4 dy \\ \\
&= \int_0^\pi y\cos(y) \left( \dfrac{16}{2} - \dfrac{4}{2} \right) dy \\ \\
&= 6 \int_0^\pi y\cos(y) \, dy
\end{align}$

We'll use integration by parts to evaluate the outer integral. Let $u = y$ and $dv = \cos(y) \, dy$. Then $du = dy$ and $v = \sin(y)$.

$\begin{align}
& 6 \displaystyle \int_0^\pi y\cos(y) \, dy \\ \\
&= 6y \sin(y) \bigg|_0^\pi -6 \int_0^\pi \sin(y) \, dy \\ \\
&= 6(\pi \sin(\pi) - 0 \sin(0)) + 6\cos(y) \bigg|_0^\pi \\ \\
&= 0 + 6(-1 - 1) \\ \\
&= -12
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_0^\pi \left( \int_2^{4} xy\cos(y) \, dx \right) dy = -12$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \left( \int_{-2}^2 \dfrac{x + y}{x^2} \, dy \right) dx =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \left( \int_{-2}^2 \dfrac{x + y}{x^2} \, dy \right) dx &= \displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \left( \int_{-2}^2 \dfrac{1}{x} + \dfrac{y}{x^2} \, dy \right) dx \\ \\
&= \displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \left[ \dfrac{y}{x} + \dfrac{y^2}{2x^2} \right]_{-2}^2 dx \\ \\
&= \displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \dfrac{2}{x} + \dfrac{4}{2x^2} - \dfrac{-2}{x} - \dfrac{4}{2x^2} \, dx \\ \\
&= \displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \dfrac{4}{x} \, dx
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \dfrac{4}{x} \, dx &= 4 \ln(x) \bigg|_1^{e^2} \\ \\
&= 4 \ln \left( e^2 \right) - 4 \ln(1) \\ \\
&= 8
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_1^{e^2} \left( \int_{-2}^2 \dfrac{x + y}{x^2} \, dy \right) dx = 8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^1 \left( \int_e^{e^5} \dfrac{x^2}{y} \, dy \right) dx =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_0^1 \left( \int_e^{e^5} \dfrac{x^2}{y} \, dy \right) dx &= \int_0^1 \left[ x^2 \ln (y) \right]_e^{e^5} dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 x^2 (\ln(e^5) - \ln(e)) \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 4x^2 \, dx
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_0^1 \left( \int_e^{e^5} \dfrac{x^2}{y} \, dy \right) dx = \dfrac{4}{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the iterated integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_1^4 \left( \int_1^2 \dfrac{x^2 + 2y}{x} \, dy \right) dx =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Evaluate the inner integral:

$\begin{align}
&\displaystyle \int_1^4 \left( \int_1^2 \dfrac{x^2 + 2y}{x} \, dy \right) dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^4 \left[ xy + \dfrac{y^2}{x} \right]_1^2 dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^4 \left( 2x + \dfrac{4}{x} \right) - \left( x + \dfrac{1}{x} \right) dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^4 x + \dfrac{3}{x} \, dx
\end{align}$

Evaluate the outer integral:

$\begin{align}
\displaystyle \int_1^4 x + \dfrac{3}{x} \, dx &= \dfrac{x^2}{2} + 3 \ln(x) \bigg|_1^4 \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{16}{2} + 3\ln(4) \right) - \left( \dfrac{1}{2} + 3 \ln(1) \right) \\ \\
&= \dfrac{15}{2} + 3\ln(4)
\end{align}$

The answer:

$\displaystyle \int_1^4 \left( \int_1^2 \dfrac{x^2 + 2y}{x} \, dy \right) dx = \dfrac{15}{2} + 3\ln(4)$
